commands.review_summary:
Adds non-negative finite `maxBytes` validation in the media-core response prefix reader and regression coverage for invalid response/snippet limits.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +19. Total +26 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection. Current main only compares response byte counts with `maxBytes`, so `NaN` and `Infinity` bypass the intended cap; the PR body also includes after-fix live output for the invalid-limit cases.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The code repair is concrete and no automated fix is needed; the remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance of the SDK-visible fail-closed behavior.

target.title: fix(media): reject invalid response byte limits
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`readResponseWithLimit` enforced response body size by comparing incoming chunks against `maxBytes`, but it did not validate that `maxBytes` itself was finite. Passing `NaN` made every `nextTotal > maxBytes` comparison false, so the byte cap was silently disabled. `Infinity` also turned the hard cap into an unbounded read.

The async byte stream helper already rejects invalid byte limits; this brings the response-body path in line with that contract.

## Why This Change Was Made

Response reads are used as a media safety boundary. A non-finite or negative limit should fail closed before reading the body instead of accidentally accepting an unbounded response. The validation is placed in the shared prefix reader so both `readResponseWithLimit` and `readResponseTextSnippet` get the same guard.

## User Impact

Callers that accidentally pass invalid response byte limits now get an immediate, explicit `RangeError` instead of losing the intended download cap.

## Evidence

Live behavior proof from this branch:

```text
{"api":"readResponseWithLimit","maxBytes":"NaN","error":"maxBytes must be a non-negative finite number: NaN"}
{"api":"readResponseWithLimit","maxBytes":"Infinity","error":"maxBytes must be a non-negative finite number: Infinity"}
{"api":"readResponseWithLimit","maxBytes":"-1","error":"maxBytes must be a non-negative finite number: -1"}
{"api":"readResponseTextSnippet","maxBytes":"NaN","error":"maxBytes must be a non-negative finite number: NaN"}
```

Focused validation:

```text
RUN  v4.1.8 C:/Users/lin20/Documents/New project 3/openclaw

Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  15 passed (15)
  Start at  20:39:13
  Duration  962ms (transform 629ms, setup 530ms, import 42ms, tests 143ms, environment 0ms)
```

```text
Checking formatting...

All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 1787ms on 2 files using 32 threads.
```

`git diff --check` produced no output.

AI-assisted: OpenAI Codex helped identify the response-limit edge case and prepare the focused regression coverage.
